'''Corutarn''' was a celestial body<ref name="Polyhedron">{{PolyhedronCite|90|The New Republic Campaign: The Battle for Endor from the PCs' Perspective}}</ref> located in the [[Corutarn system/Legends|Corutarn system]], which was a part of the [[Arrowhead]], or [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="TEA" /> The [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Perrn]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]],<ref name="Polyhedron" /> who was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] around [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="Date">{{PolyhedronCite|90|The New Republic Campaign: The Battle for Endor from the PCs' Perspective}} establishes that [[Perrn]] was thirty-six [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old at the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], which translates to an approximate [[Reproduction/Legends|birth]] year of [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> [[Homeworld/Legends|hailed]] from Corutarn.<ref name="Polyhedron" />

==Behind the scenes==
Corutarn was mentioned in "[[The New Republic Campaign: The Battle for Endor from the PCs' Perspective]]," a [[roleplaying game]] source article authored by [[Bill Slavicsek]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in the [[Polyhedron 90|ninetieth issue]] of the ''[[Polyhedron]]'' magazine in [[December]] [[1993]]. Since that article was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="Polyhedron" /> it was considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Corutarn [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore Corutarn itself, in grid square M-10.<ref name="TEA" />
